scope: Enforce contract version validity (Valid/Signed by both sides) in Mass Email, Mass SMS, and Penalty Calculation after product/service contract versioning (PDT-2599). Primary requirements in attachment 'Using validity in 3 processes.docx' and Jira description; linked issue PDT-2599 (Done).

upstream.name: product_contract.contract_details (versionStatus/status, start_date, end_date)
upstream.usage: Product contract version rows; SIGNED used as 'Valid' in several queries

upstream.name: service_contract.contract_details (contractVersionStatus, start_date, end_date)
upstream.usage: Service contract version rows; ContractVersionStatus.SIGNED = Valid in UI mapping

upstream.name: product_contract.contracts / service_contract.contracts (contract_status, sub_status)
upstream.usage: Header-level signed-by-both-sides checks in some mass-comm and billing queries

upstream.name: PDT-2599 service contract versioning
upstream.usage: Linked Jira PDT-2599 — version status on service contracts; parent change for validity enforcement

upstream.name: ContractDetailsStatus / ContractVersionStatus enums
upstream.usage: Domain mapping of Valid/Signed; SIGNED sub-statuses include SIGNED_BY_BOTH_SIDES

upstream.name: price_component.*, product price component links
upstream.usage: Penalty Story 3 — tag-based price component resolution from product version tied to contract version

downstream.name: CRM Mass Email/SMS customer-contact persistence
downstream.usage: Wrong contract detail version → wrong recipients and communication-customer links

downstream.name: Action penalty amount, documents, liabilities
downstream.usage: Wrong version context → wrong consumption, term dates, PAV, price components, currency/min-max

downstream.name: Mass communication import error reports
downstream.usage: Must surface backend rejection when version not eligible (Jira TO-BE)

downstream.name: EnergoTS / portal Mass Email & Mass SMS screens
downstream.usage: FE should display import rejection messages; no payload redesign per attachment

integration_points: Mass Email contract import: explicit version vs latest-by-startDate fallback
Mass SMS contract import: same resolution paths as Mass Email
Penalty: execution-date version resolution (product vs service parity)
Penalty: POD scope for formulas 2/4/6/8 vs respective version only
Penalty: price component tag filtering (0 / 1 / many components)
Penalty: version-scoped variables (consumption, term dates, PAV, parameters, currency)
Cross-process consistency: same contract+date should pick same eligible version

what_could_break.reason: Adding SIGNED/contractVersionStatus filter will reject rows that currently resolve
Changing from max(startDate) to max eligible SIGNED version changes selected recipients
Service branch lacks contractVersionStatus=SIGNED; aligning may shrink recipient set vs product-only SIGNED filter
Adding contractVersionStatus=SIGNED may return null where penalties currently calculate
Narrowing from respective+future to respective-only breaks actions that attach PODs from future versions
Uses respective+future POD counts; TO-BE may require respective-version-only counts
Stricter version filter changes amounts for same execution date unless historical guard enforced

technical_details: Jira PDT-2815 (Customer Feedback, Testing): summary 'Changes in processes impacted by version validity'. Description: Mass Email, Mass SMS, and Penalty Calculation do not consistently consider contract version validity; expected consistent date-resolved version, version-scoped penalty variables/PODs, clear validation errors, stable historical traceability. customfield_10103 empty (media only); customfield_10048/10217 empty. Attachment 'Using validity in 3 processes.docx' (PDT-2599 follow-on) defines three stories: (1) Mass Email — only Valid/Signed-by-both-sides versions, explicit version must be eligible, latest eligible fallback, reject with report reason; (2) Mass SMS — same; (3) Penalty — resolve version by execution date + Valid/Signed, version-scoped variables, POD filter for formulas 2/4/6/8, price component tag rules, historical consistency. Linked PDT-2599 (Done): service contract version status. Code (dev-aligned read-only): Mass comm import accepts any existing versionId without status check; latest fallback uses max(startDate) only. All-customers: product CTE filters pccd.status='SIGNED'; service CTE does not filter contract_version_status. Penalty product getContractDetailIdByExecutionDate filters cd.status='SIGNED'; service equivalent does not filter contractVersionStatus. Penalty variable SQL uses date window on contract_details without SIGNED on product penalty query join; service penalty query has no SIGNED. validatePods allows PODs from respective OR future versions. Price component tag errors already match attachment text in PenaltyFormulaEvaluationService.

description_vs_code_gaps.area: Mass Email — explicit contract version import
description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: If contract number + version is provided, that version must be in status Valid/Signed by both sides; otherwise row rejected.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: processContractInfoVersionId loads projection by contractNumber + versionId only; no versionStatus/contractVersionStatus filter.

description_vs_code_gaps.area: Mass SMS — explicit contract version import
description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Same eligibility and rejection as Mass Email for explicit import version.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: SmsCommunicationService.processContractInfoVersionId mirrors email path without status validation.

description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Without explicit version, resolve latest version in status Valid/Signed by both sides only.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: findLatestProductContractDetailVersionId / findLatestServiceContractDetailVersionId select max(startDate) with no SIGNED/contractVersionStatus predicate.

description_vs_code_gaps.area: Mass Email/SMS — all customers with active contract
description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: All-customers mode must apply same eligibility (Valid/Signed) across product and service contract types.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: fetchActiveContractsAndAssociatedCustomersForMassCommunication filters product details with pccd.status='SIGNED' but service_contract_details CTE has no contract_version_status=SIGNED filter.

description_vs_code_gaps.area: Penalty — service contract version by execution date
description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Resolve respective version with status Valid/Signed by both sides and date window; if none, penalty not calculated with validation error.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: ServiceContractDetailsRepository.getContractDetailIdByExecutionDate filters contract ACTIVE and date range only; no contractVersionStatus=SIGNED.

description_vs_code_gaps.area: Penalty — product contract version by execution date (parity)
description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Same Valid/Signed enforcement for product and service.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: Product getContractDetailIdByExecutionDate includes cd.status='SIGNED' in subquery — closer to Jira for product only.

description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: All version-scoped variables read only from resolved Valid/Signed version context.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: getPenaltyCalculationVariablesForProductContract joins contract_details by execution date window with c.status='ACTIVE' but does not require cd.status='SIGNED' in main join (unlike getContractDetailIdByExecutionDate).

description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Version-scoped penalty variables from eligible signed version only.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: getPenaltyCalculationVariablesForServiceContract uses date window on contract_details without contractVersionStatus=SIGNED.

description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Use only PODs belonging to the same respective contract version; ignore extra PODs from action outside that version.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: findPodsBelongingToContractForAction accepts PODs where contract_detail is respective OR any future version (start_date > respective). validatePods error text references 'respective or any future version'.

description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: None -> 'there is not valid price component'; duplicates -> 'more then 1 price component'; single -> use in calculation.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: PenaltyFormulaEvaluationService.collectPriceComponentTagValues emits matching infoErrorMessages for 0 and >1 primitive components.
description_vs_code_gaps.evidence: PenaltyFormulaEvaluationService.java ~454-458 (aligned — test should verify version-scoped productDetailId input)

description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Status label 'Valid/Signed by both sides' in BA doc.
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: DB/UI maps 'Valid' display to versionStatus/contractVersionStatus='SIGNED'; product uses contract_details.status; service uses contractVersionStatus. Sub-status SIGNED_BY_BOTH_SIDES is on contract header enums, not always applied in mass-comm queries.

description_vs_code_gaps.jira_claim: Description bullets are high-level (consistent date-resolved version, clear errors).
description_vs_code_gaps.code_behavior: Attachment specifies explicit rejection messages, formula-specific POD rules, and per-variable families; code gaps are story-specific (not fully enumerated in short description).
description_vs_code_gaps.evidence: issue-rest.json description vs Using validity in 3 processes.extracted.txt
